title: SSRF, DNS Rebinding, Injection, Response Validation, and Least Authority · Browser and Node Client Systems
description: A server-rendering image proxy and browser preview must address SSRF, DNS rebinding, CORS limits, hostile media types, and privacy-safe diagnostics. Learners cumulatively produce a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch defense-in-depth outbound request policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Allow-list required schemes, ports, hosts, and destinations and enforce network egress limits for server-side fetching. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Address DNS rebinding and validation-to-connect races by binding policy to resolved and connected destinations. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Reject control characters and unsafe dynamic structure in targets and fields and rely on maintained encoders. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
